title: Contributing to hfutils
text: Tests use testthat run small, self-contained fixtures (sf::nc sample synthetic flow networks built tests/testthat/helper-fixtures.R) — network access, cloud authentication, external command-line tools required. Run suite :

what: What coverage does and does not include
title: Contributing to hfutils
text: headline coverage number reflects package’s testable logic. Three source files intentionally excluded coverage measurement (see .covrignore) exercised headless CI run without external services: functions validated integration use rather unit tests; error-guard paths (e.g. malformed tokens) unit-tested. Everything else — /O, geometry cleaning, network/topology algorithms, versioning, staged invariant checks — expected stay well covered, please add extend tests alongside change areas.

title: Contributing to hfutils
text: Prefer behavioral assertions (outputs, invariants, error conditions) asserting specific internal branch executed. Keep fixtures small deterministic; build helper-fixtures.R can reused. User-facing errors raised cli::cli_abort().

title: Cloud access, versioning, and quality assurance
text: lynker_spatial_auth() provisions (refreshes) OAuth2 token wires libraries name. GDAL configured, as_ogr() / sf::read_sf() call can read directly cloud storage via GDAL virtual file system; DuckDB configured, bearer token attached secret.
code: # Interactive browser flow; sets GDAL_HTTP_BEARER for the session token <- lynker_spatial_auth(libs = "gdal")  as_ogr("/vsis3/lynker-spatial/hydrofabric/v2.2/conus/conus_nextgen.gpkg",        "divides")  # DuckDB over HTTP, with the token attached to the connection con <- duckdb_connection(extensions = "httpfs") tbl_http("https://.../flowpaths.parquet", conn = con)

title: Cloud access, versioning, and quality assurance
text: gpkg_set_version() stamps dataset version standard GeoPackage metadata tables — distinct GeoPackage specification version PRAGMA user_version, left untouched. records machine-readable packed integer (Mmmpp), human-readable semver string, optional SPDX license, optional JSON provenance record. idempotent: re-stamping replaces prior entries rather accumulating duplicates. int_version (20200 ) value GDAL surfaces GPKG_METADATA_ITEM_1, convenient machine comparison across releases.
code: library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  f <- tempfile(fileext = ".gpkg") nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE)[1:2, ] st_write(nc, f, "divides", quiet = TRUE)  gpkg_set_version(   f, "2.2.0",   provenance = list(software = "hydrofabric", vpuid = "09"),   license    = "ODbL-1.0" )  str(gpkg_get_version(f)) #> List of 4 #>  $ version    : chr "2.2.0" #>  $ int_version: int 20200 #>  $ license    : chr "ODbL-1.0" #>  $ provenance :List of 2 #>   ..$ software: chr "hydrofabric" #>   ..$ vpuid   : chr "09"

title: Cloud access, versioning, and quality assurance
text: hf_check_invariants() shared QA gate used stage build pipeline. Calling steps means silent failure (duplicate id, dangling toid, orphan divide, raster ring artifact) halts run step created — 200 lines later visual inspection. supports four stages — "refactored", "reconciled", "aggregated", "ngen" — expecting stage-specific inputs. topology half refactored-stage check clean toy network: default failed check throws (strict = TRUE); set strict = FALSE downgrade failures warnings still surfacing . dangling downstream pointer caught: aggregated ngen stages additionally accept coverage = TRUE run (expensive) check flowpath lies within assigned catchment.
code: refactored <- data.frame(   flowpath_id   = c("101", "102", "103"),   flowpath_toid = c("102", "103", "0") )  res <- hf_check_invariants("refactored", refactored = refactored) #> [invariants:refactored] ✓ refactored_unique                refactored flowpath_id has 0 duplicate(s) #> [invariants:refactored] ✓ refactored_topology              0 refactored flowpath_toid values reference nonexistent IDs res$ok #> [1] TRUE broken <- data.frame(   flowpath_id   = c("101", "102"),   flowpath_toid = c("102", "999")   # 999 does not exist )  hf_check_invariants("refactored", refactored = broken) #> [invariants:refactored] ✓ refactored_unique                refactored flowpath_id has 0 duplicate(s) #> Error: #> ! [invariants:refactored] ✗ refactored_topology              1 refactored flowpath_toid values reference nonexistent IDs

title: Network properties: accumulation and hydrosequence
text: accumulate_downstream() propagates per-feature attribute (drainage area, incremental length, anything additive) downstream, summing upstream contributions confluences. runs single topological sort followed one O(E) edge pass, scales continental networks. Consider two headwaters (1, 2) joining 3, flows outlet 4: Feature 3 accumulates 1.0 + 2.0 + 0.5 = 3.5, outlet inherits full basin total. column names default flowpath_id / flowpath_toid can overridden via id toid arguments match network table (including reference-fabric id/toid). cyclic network rejected rather silently producing wrong totals:
code: df <- data.frame(   flowpath_id   = c("1", "2", "3", "4"),   flowpath_toid = c("3", "3", "4", "0"),   area          = c(1.0, 2.0, 0.5, 0.0) )  accumulate_downstream(df, attr = "area") #> [1] 1.0 2.0 3.5 3.5 cyclic <- data.frame(   flowpath_id   = c("1", "2"),   flowpath_toid = c("2", "1"),   area          = c(1, 1) )  accumulate_downstream(cyclic, attr = "area") #> Error in `accumulate_downstream()`: #> ! Network contains cycles; cannot accumulate.

title: Network properties: accumulation and hydrosequence
text: get_hydroseq() assigns total topological ordering network. Larger values upstream; value decreases monotonically move toward outlet, makes convenient sort key downstream traversal.
code: df$hydroseq <- get_hydroseq(df) df[order(-df$hydroseq), c("flowpath_id", "flowpath_toid", "hydroseq")] #>   flowpath_id flowpath_toid hydroseq #> 4           4             0        4 #> 2           2             3        3 #> 1           1             3        2 #> 3           3             4        1

title: Network properties: accumulation and hydrosequence
text: functions accept data frame, tibble, sf object. typical pattern reads layer lazily, filters VPU, materializes , accumulates: See vignette("reading--writing") /O side pipeline.
code: library(dplyr)  da <- as_ogr("conus_nextgen.gpkg", "flowpaths") |>   filter(vpuid == "01") |>   st_as_sf() |>   accumulate_downstream(attr = "areasqkm")

title: Reading and writing a hydrofabric
text: read_hydrofabric() auto-detects flowpath divide layers name, can harmonize reproject coordinate reference systems read. stage tiny two-layer GeoPackage read back: read_hydrofabric() also accepts -memory sf objects directly (skip GeoPackage), realization argument read "divides" "flowpaths", crs argument transform outputs way .
code: library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  # Stand-in geometries (the North Carolina sample shipped with sf) nc <- st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE)[1:5, ]  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gpkg") write_hydrofabric(   list(divides = nc, flowpaths = st_cast(nc, "MULTILINESTRING")),   outfile = tmp,   verbose = FALSE )  hf <- read_hydrofabric(tmp, verbose = FALSE) names(hf) #> [1] "flowpaths" "divides" nrow(hf$divides) #> [1] 5 fp_only <- read_hydrofabric(tmp, realization = "flowpaths", crs = 5070,                             verbose = FALSE) st_crs(fp_only$flowpaths)$epsg #> [1] 5070

title: Reading and writing a hydrofabric
text: as_ogr() returns lazy table can manipulate dplyr verbs; nothing read collect() (data frame) st_as_sf() (spatial). efficient way pull single VPU continental file without loading whole layer: GDAL backs connection, call works cloud objects via GDAL virtual file systems — see vignette("cloud--versioning") authenticated /vsis3/ access.
code: library(dplyr)  as_ogr("conus_nextgen.gpkg", "divides") |>   filter(vpuid == "01") |>   select(divide_id, areasqkm) |>   st_as_sf()

what: Accumulate an attribute downstream over a directed acyclic network — accumulate_downstream
title: Accumulate an attribute downstream over a directed acyclic network — accumulate_downstream
text: Propagates per-node attribute (e.g. area) downstream along `toid` links returns accumulated (upstream-summed) value input row. network must DAG (cycles). O(E) pass single topological sort fast large hydro networks.

title: Accumulate an attribute downstream over a directed acyclic network — accumulate_downstream
Accumulate an attribute downstream over a directed acyclic network — accumulate_downstream
text: x data frame, tibble, sf object containing least identifier column given `id`, downstream pointer column given `toid`, attribute column named `attr`. id Character scalar. Column name `x` unique node identifiers. Defaults `"flowpath_id"`. toid Character scalar. Column name `x` *downstream* node identifier row. Use `NA` `0` outlets/terminals. Defaults `"flowpath_toid"`. attr Character scalar. Column name `x` containing attribute accumulate (e.g., `"incremental_areasqkm"`). Values coerced numeric.

title: Accumulate an attribute downstream over a directed acyclic network — accumulate_downstream
text: numeric vector length `nrow(x)` giving accumulated   totals aligned rows `x`.

title: Accumulate an attribute downstream over a directed acyclic network — accumulate_downstream
text: algorithm builds vertex set `id` non-missing `toid` values, performs topological sort (using igraph), processes edges nondecreasing topological order sources. edge `u -> v`, running total `u` added `v`. naturally handles confluences multiple upstream sources contribute downstream target. function treats `NA` `0` `toid` outlets. function stops error network contains cycles. Ensure graph acyclic (tree/DAG) calling.

title: Accumulate an attribute downstream over a directed acyclic network — accumulate_downstream
text: single neighbor-independent edge pass made topo sort, computations done integer indices. avoids per-vertex neighbor lookups scales well large hydrologic networks.

title: Accumulate an attribute downstream over a directed acyclic network — accumulate_downstream
code: # Toy: 1 -> 3, 2 -> 3, 3 -> 4 (two headwaters merging into 3, then into 4) df <- data.frame(   flowpath_id   = c(1, 2, 3, 4),   flowpath_toid = c(3, 3, 4, NA),   area           = c(1.0, 2.0, 0.5, 0.0) ) accumulate_downstream(df, id = "flowpath_id", toid = "flowpath_toid", attr = "area") #> [1] 1.0 2.0 3.5 3.5 # Expected: node 1 stays 1.0, node 2 stays 2.0, # node 3 gets 1.0 + 2.0 + 0.5 = 3.5, node 4 gets 3.5 + 0.0 = 3.5

title: Clean Catchment Geometry — clean_geometry
text: Fix common geometry issues DEM-derived catchments (disjoint fragments, artifacts, invalid topologies) optionally apply topology- preserving simplification via mapshaper. `flowlines` provided, largest part per catchment chosen preferentially touches corresponding flowline feature.

title: Clean Catchment Geometry — clean_geometry
code: clean_geometry(   catchments,   flowlines = NULL,   fl_ID = NULL,   ID = "ID",   keep = NULL,   crs = 5070,   grid = 9e-04,   gb = 8,   force = FALSE,   sys = NULL )

title: Clean Catchment Geometry — clean_geometry
text: catchments sf POLYGON/MULTIPOLYGON unique ID column. flowlines optional sf LINESTRING/MULTILINESTRING picking "prime" part per catchment (largest piece intersects flowline). fl_ID character. Unique identifier column `flowlines` matches `ID` `catchments`. ID character. Unique identifier column `catchments`. Default: "ID". keep numeric (0,1]. Proportion points retain simplification. `NULL`, simplification performed. Default: `NULL`. crs integer object accepted `sf::st_crs`. projected CRS suitable area calculations. Default: 5070 (NAD83 / Conus Albers). grid numeric. Snap--grid size (target CRS units). Default: 0.0009. gb integer. Heap GB `mapshaper-xl` `force = TRUE` `gb > 8`. Default: 8. force logical. Use system `mapshaper` / `mapshaper-xl` binaries directly simplification (faster robust large data). Default: FALSE. sys logical NULL. Use system mapshaper rmapshaper calls. NULL, auto-detect. Set env `TURN_OFF_SYS_MAPSHAPER=YUP` force `FALSE`.

title: Clean Catchment Geometry — clean_geometry
text: sf ID column fixed geometry; includes `areasqkm`.

what: Clean polygon topology in a GeoPackage layer using GDAL and Mapshaper — clean_gpkg_layer
title: Clean polygon topology in a GeoPackage layer using GDAL and Mapshaper — clean_gpkg_layer
text: Extracts polygon layer GeoPackage, reprojects EPSG:4326, runs `mapshaper -clean` repair polygon topology, reprojects cleaned output back original coordinate reference system, overwrites original layer source GeoPackage.

title: Clean polygon topology in a GeoPackage layer using GDAL and Mapshaper — clean_gpkg_layer
code: clean_gpkg_layer(   gpkg,   layer,   mapshaper = "mapshaper",   ogr2ogr = "ogr2ogr",   gdalsrsinfo = "gdalsrsinfo",   snap_interval = NULL,   gap_fill_area = NULL,   overlap_rule = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Clean polygon topology in a GeoPackage layer using GDAL and Mapshaper — clean_gpkg_layer
text: gpkg Character scalar. Path input GeoPackage. layer Character scalar. Name layer `gpkg` clean. mapshaper Character scalar. Path `mapshaper` executable. Defaults `"mapshaper"`, assuming available system path. ogr2ogr Character scalar. Path `ogr2ogr` executable. Defaults `"ogr2ogr"`. gdalsrsinfo Character scalar. Path `gdalsrsinfo` executable. Defaults `"gdalsrsinfo"`. snap_interval Optional numeric character scalar. Passed `mapshaper -clean` `snap-interval=...`. Use snap nearby vertices topology repair. cleaning occurs EPSG:4326, value interpreted decimal degrees. gap_fill_area Optional numeric character scalar. Passed `mapshaper -clean` `gap-fill-area=...`. Controls maximum enclosed gap area filled. overlap_rule Optional character scalar. Passed `mapshaper -clean` `overlap-rule=...`. Controls overlap areas assigned. Common values include `"max-area"`, `"min-area"`, `"max-id"`, `"min-id"`. verbose Logical scalar. `TRUE`, print step--step progress, commands, captured CRS detection output.

title: Clean polygon topology in a GeoPackage layer using GDAL and Mapshaper — clean_gpkg_layer
text: Invisibly returns named list : gpkg Path GeoPackage modified. layer Layer name cleaned. original_srs CRS definition used project cleaned layer   back original coordinate system. tmp_files Named character vector temporary intermediate files   used processing. scheduled deletion exit.

title: Clean polygon topology in a GeoPackage layer using GDAL and Mapshaper — clean_gpkg_layer
text: function intended polygon datasets may contain small overlaps, enclosed gaps, minor boundary misalignments prevent clean polygon mosaic. topology repair performed geographic coordinates `mapshaper -clean` often reliable mixed-source inputs, data restored original CRS. function relies external command line tools: `ogr2ogr` layer extraction, reprojection, overwrite `gdalsrsinfo` recovering original CRS definition `mapshaper` polygon cleaning CRS detection prefers EPSG code, PROJ string, finally simple WKT representation fallback. processing steps : Extract requested layer source GeoPackage     temporary single-layer GeoPackage. Detect original CRS using `gdalsrsinfo`, preferring EPSG output. Reproject temporary layer EPSG:4326 write GeoJSON. Run `mapshaper -clean` EPSG:4326 GeoJSON. Reproject cleaned output back original CRS. Overwrite original layer input GeoPackage. Temporary files created `tempdir()` removed `.exit()`. function assumes target layer contains polygonal data appropriate `mapshaper -clean`. may still run geometry types, repair behavior designed polygon topology.

title: Clean polygon topology in a GeoPackage layer using GDAL and Mapshaper — clean_gpkg_layer
text: following executables must installed available: GDAL tools: `ogr2ogr`, `gdalsrsinfo` Mapshaper CLI many systems, Mapshaper can installed :

title: Clean polygon topology in a GeoPackage layer using GDAL and Mapshaper — clean_gpkg_layer
text: Cleaning performed EPSG:4326, `snap_interval` interpreted   angular units, source layer's native units. Overwriting replaces original layer GeoPackage. Attribute preservation depends behavior `ogr2ogr`   `mapshaper`; field ordering metadata may change. large layers may require substantial temporary disk space.

title: Clean polygon topology in a GeoPackage layer using GDAL and Mapshaper — clean_gpkg_layer
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ clean_gpkg_layer(   gpkg = "data/hydrofabric.gpkg",   layer = "divides" )  clean_gpkg_layer(   gpkg = "data/hydrofabric.gpkg",   layer = "catchments",   snap_interval = 1e-7,   overlap_rule = "max-area",   verbose = TRUE )  clean_gpkg_layer(   gpkg = "data/hydrofabric.gpkg",   layer = "basins",   gap_fill_area = 0,   mapshaper = "/usr/local/bin/mapshaper",   ogr2ogr = "/opt/homebrew/bin/ogr2ogr",   gdalsrsinfo = "/opt/homebrew/bin/gdalsrsinfo" ) } # }

Defaults `"flowpath_id"`. toid Character scalar. Column name `topology` *downstream* node identifier row. Use `NA` `0` outlets/terminals. Defaults `"flowpath_toid"`.

code: # 1 -> 2 -> 3 (outlet). Headwater "1" gets the largest hydroseq. df <- data.frame(   flowpath_id   = c("1", "2", "3"),   flowpath_toid = c("2", "3", "0") ) get_hydroseq(df) #> [1] 2 1 3

what: Stamp a dataset version into a GeoPackage's metadata tables — gpkg_set_version
title: Stamp a dataset version into a GeoPackage's metadata tables — gpkg_set_version
text: Records dataset version standard GeoPackage metadata extension tables (`gpkg_metadata` / `gpkg_metadata_reference`), creating registering extension GeoPackage already . Three entries written `geopackage` scope:

title: Stamp a dataset version into a GeoPackage's metadata tables — gpkg_set_version
code: gpkg_set_version(   gpkg,   version,   int_version = semver_to_int(version),   provenance = NULL,   license = NULL,   scope = "geopackage" )

title: Stamp a dataset version into a GeoPackage's metadata tables — gpkg_set_version
text: gpkg Path GeoPackage file. version Human-readable semantic version, e.g. `"2.1.4"`. int_version Machine-readable integer version. Defaults packed `Mmmpp` form `version`. provenance Optional named `list` written JSON build-provenance entry (example `list(software = "hydrofabric 0.1", git_sha = "...", build_date = "...")`). `NULL` (default) writes provenance entry. license Optional SPDX license identifier (e.g. `"CC0-1.0"`, `"ODbL-1.0"`) recorded metadata entry. `NULL` (default) writes none. scope GeoPackage metadata reference scope. Default `"geopackage"` (whole-dataset).

title: Stamp a dataset version into a GeoPackage's metadata tables — gpkg_set_version
text: `gpkg` path, invisibly.

title: Stamp a dataset version into a GeoPackage's metadata tables — gpkg_set_version
text: machine-readable integer version (`Mmmpp`, `text/plain`), surfaced     GDAL `GPKG_METADATA_ITEM_1`; human-readable semantic version (`text/plain`); `provenance` supplied, JSON build-provenance record     (`application/json`). dataset version independent GeoPackage *specification* version held `PRAGMA user_version`, left untouched. function idempotent: re-stamping replaces prior entries wrote rather accumulating duplicates.

what: Physical-range bounds for divide and flowpath attributes — hf_attr_bounds
title: Physical-range bounds for divide and flowpath attributes — hf_attr_bounds
text: Returns attribute plausibility-bounds table shipped package (`inst/extdata/divide_attr_bounds.csv`). row gives physically reasonable range divide flowpath modeling parameter. `caveat` column flags bounds demonstrably strict look like placeholders; excluded checks default. `aliases` column (`;`-separated) lists alternate column names bound also matches, single bound covers schema naming differences (e.g. `lengthkm` matches `length_km`).

title: Physical-range bounds for divide and flowpath attributes — hf_attr_bounds
Physical-range bounds for divide and flowpath attributes — hf_attr_bounds
text: data.frame bounds (one row per attribute, plus per-domain rows   `lat`/`lon`).

what: Check divide/flowpath attribute values against physical-range bounds — hf_check_attr_bounds
title: Check divide/flowpath attribute values against physical-range bounds — hf_check_attr_bounds
text: Complements topology/geometry checks [hf_check_invariants()] per-attribute physical-plausibility pass: every modeling parameter present layer checked expected range. Attributes present layer skipped; bounds carrying `caveat` skipped unless `trust_caveated = TRUE`. Failures reported never abort, even `strict = TRUE`.

title: Check divide/flowpath attribute values against physical-range bounds — hf_check_attr_bounds
code: hf_check_attr_bounds(   layer,   which = c("divides", "flowpaths"),   domain = NULL,   strict = FALSE,   trust_caveated = FALSE,   max_report = 5L )

title: Check divide/flowpath attribute values against physical-range bounds — hf_check_attr_bounds
text: layer data.frame sf object (Divides Flowpaths layer). One `"divides"` `"flowpaths"`. domain Domain code `lat`/`lon` bounds: one `"CONUS"`, `"AK"`, `"HI"`, `"PRVI"`, `NULL` skip lat/lon. strict Logical. Passed reporter; attribute-bound failures soft warn regardless. trust_caveated Logical. Include bounds flagged `caveat` column (default `FALSE`). max_report Integer. Max example offending values show per attribute.

title: Check divide/flowpath attribute values against physical-range bounds — hf_check_attr_bounds
text: Invisibly list `ok` `checks` (shape   [hf_check_invariants()]).

title: Pipeline invariant checks — hf_check_invariants
text: Runs set invariant assertions named pipeline stage. Designed called pipeline steps silent failures (orphan divides, unsplit parents, raster artifacts) halt run step created problem – 200 lines later visual inspection.

title: Pipeline invariant checks — hf_check_invariants
code: hf_check_invariants(   stage,   ...,   strict = TRUE,   coverage = FALSE,   coverage_min = 0.9,   attr_bounds = FALSE,   domain = NULL,   attr_trust_caveated = FALSE )

title: Pipeline invariant checks — hf_check_invariants
text: stage Character. One `"refactored"`, `"reconciled"`, `"aggregated"`, `"ngen"`. ... Stage-specific named arguments (see details). strict Logical. `TRUE` (default) failed check throws error. `FALSE`, failures become warnings. coverage Logical. `TRUE`, run expensive flowpath--catchment coverage check (aggregated ngen stages ). Default `FALSE`. coverage_min Minimum fraction flowpath must lie inside assigned catchment. Default `0.90`. attr_bounds Logical. `TRUE`, append per-attribute physical-range plausibility pass `divides` `flowpaths` layers (see [hf_check_attr_bounds()]). Soft/warn-default, never changes returned `ok` existing callers enabled. Absent attribute columns skipped. domain Domain code (`"CONUS"`, `"AK"`, `"HI"`, `"PRVI"`) selecting `lat`/`lon` bounds attribute pass; `NULL` skips lat/lon. attr_trust_caveated Logical. Include attribute bounds flagged `caveat` column bounds table (default `FALSE`).

title: Pipeline invariant checks — hf_check_invariants
text: Invisibly list `ok` (logical), `stage`, `checks`   (named list per-check results).

title: Pipeline invariant checks — hf_check_invariants
text: Expected arguments per stage: - `stage = "refactored"`: `refactored` (sf, split NHD flowlines),   `reconciled` (sf, collapsed--reconciled lines integer `ID`). - `stage = "reconciled"`: `reconciled` (sf), `divides` (sf reconciled   divides). - `stage = "aggregated"`: `flowpaths` (sf), `divides` (sf), `network`   (data.frame, optional). - `stage = "ngen"`: `flowpaths` (sf `fp-` IDs), `divides` (sf   `cat-` IDs).

what: hfutils: Core Utilities for the Hydrofabric Stack — hfutils-package
title: hfutils: Core Utilities for the Hydrofabric Stack — hfutils-package
text: Base-layer utilities accessing, cleaning, summarizing, versioning hydrofabric datasets. Provides hydrofabric /O (GeoPackage, GeoParquet, lazy GDAL/OGR-SQL access), DEM-derived geometry cleaning, network topology algorithms (downstream accumulation, hydrosequence), staged pipeline invariant checks, GeoPackage dataset-version provenance metadata helpers used across hydrofabric package suite.

title: hfutils: Core Utilities for the Hydrofabric Stack — hfutils-package

what: Read Hydrofabric Layers from a GeoPackage (or accept in-memory `sf` objects) — read_hydrofabric
title: Read Hydrofabric Layers from a GeoPackage (or accept in-memory `sf` objects) — read_hydrofabric
text: Convenience reader Hydrofabric GeoPackage returns named list `sf` objects: `list(divides = ..., flowpaths = ...)`. may provide GeoPackage path let function auto-detect layer names, ** pass -memory `sf` objects directly. Optionally transforms target CRS harmonizes CRS across returned layers.

title: Read Hydrofabric Layers from a GeoPackage (or accept in-memory `sf` objects) — read_hydrofabric
code: read_hydrofabric(   gpkg = NULL,   divides = NULL,   flowpaths = NULL,   realization = c("all", "divides", "flowpaths"),   crs = NULL,   verbose = Sys.getenv("hydrofab_verbose") != "false" )

title: Read Hydrofabric Layers from a GeoPackage (or accept in-memory `sf` objects) — read_hydrofabric
text: gpkg Character path GeoPackage. `NULL`, function use `divides` /`flowpaths` arguments (provided). Default: `NULL`. divides Either `NULL`, character layer name within `gpkg`, -memory `sf` object representing divides/catchments. flowpaths Either `NULL`, character layer name within `gpkg`, -memory `sf` object representing flowpaths/flowlines. realization One `""`, `"divides"`, `"flowpaths"`. Controls layers auto-discover/read `gpkg` corresponding arguments `NULL`. Default: `""`. crs Optional CRS transform outputs (e.g., EPSG code like `5070`, `sf::crs` object, input accepted `sf::st_crs()`). verbose Logical; print progress messages. Default uses environment variable `hydrofab_verbose` (anything literal `"false"` treated verbose).

title: Read Hydrofabric Layers from a GeoPackage (or accept in-memory `sf` objects) — read_hydrofabric
text: named list two elements: `divides`: `sf` object (available) `flowpaths`: `sf` object (available)

title: Read Hydrofabric Layers from a GeoPackage (or accept in-memory `sf` objects) — read_hydrofabric
text: - `divides`/`flowpaths` provided -memory `sf` objects,   used -(subject optional CRS transformation). - `gpkg` provided corresponding layer argument : `NULL`: function tries auto-detect layer name. character: name used (validated). - Auto-discovery looks : Flowpaths: names matching `flowpath|flowline`, excluding       `attributes|edge_list`. Divides: names matching `divide|catchment`, excluding `network`. multiple candidates found, error thrown candidates listed. CRS behavior: - `crs` provided, returned layers transformed CRS. - Otherwise, two layers returned different CRSs, second   transformed CRS first.

title: Read Hydrofabric Layers from a GeoPackage (or accept in-memory `sf` objects) — read_hydrofabric
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Auto-detect layers from a HydroFabric GPKG and return both x <- read_hydrofabric("path/to/hydrofabric.gpkg")  # Read only flowpaths (auto-detected) and transform to EPSG:5070 x <- read_hydrofabric("path/to/hydrofabric.gpkg", realization = "flowpaths", crs = 5070)  # Supply in-memory sf layers (no gpkg), harmonize to EPSG:3857 x <- read_hydrofabric(divides = my_divides_sf, flowpaths = my_flow_sf, crs = 3857) } # }

title: hfutils 0.3.4
text: Correctness quality pass base layer. tbl_http() longer errors default call — malformed read_func default fixed validated match.arg(). get_hydroseq() now character-safe: non-numeric identifiers ("fp-", scientific-notation strings) round-trip instead coerced NA. write_hydrofabric() defaults enforce_dm = FALSE; data-model schema (hf_dm) consulted caller supplies , default call longer fails looking object hfutils ship. Fixed union_polygons() erroring (object '.' found) disjoint-MULTIPOLYGON dedup branch — used magrittr . pronoun native |> pipe. path fires exactly group unions disjoint parts; now regression-tested. Removed divergent duplicate definitions rename_geometry(), add_areasqkm(), fast_validity_check() (kept one canonical copy ). create_metadata() longer warns every GeoParquet write (now opt-via quiet = FALSE); fixed misspelled licence metadata key. New vignettes: Reading writing hydrofabric, Network properties, Cloud access, versioning, quality assurance. Grouped pkgdown reference index; package R CMD check clean (NOTEs). Continuous integration: added R-CMD-check matrix (macOS/Windows/Ubuntu, R release/devel/oldrel) test-coverage (covr/Codecov) workflow, badges. pkgdown site now builds deploys docs/. Test coverage package’s testable logic ~79% (core files 75-99%). Three files require external services – clean_gpkg_layer() (GDAL/ mapshaper CLIs), duckdb/tbl_http() (live DuckDB + cloud), OAuth flow auth.R – excluded measurement via .covrignore documented CONTRIBUTING.md. New suites hf_check_invariants() (four stages incl. reconciled flow-direction, coverage, fp->nexus->fp DAG checks, strict/non-strict), get_hydroseq(), write_hydrofabric()/read_hydrofabric() round trip (incl. mixed sf + attribute-table layers), clean_geometry() (single-part, multi-part, flowline-driven, simplification), union_polygons() / union_linestrings() / flowpaths_to_linestrings(), small measure/node helpers, lazy as_ogr() OGR-SQL backend (named layer, raw query, multi-layer), GeoParquet round trips, auth guard paths. Standardized user-facing errors write_hydrofabric() as_ogr() cli::cli_abort().

title: hfutils 0.3.3
text: Add gpkg_set_version() / gpkg_get_version() — stamp read dataset version (machine integer Mmmpp + human semver) standard GeoPackage metadata extension tables, optional build-provenance JSON entry SPDX license. Idempotent; leaves GeoPackage spec version (user_version) untouched. Add hf_check_invariants() — shared staged pipeline invariant checks (refactored / reconciled / aggregated / ngen) every layer stack can use one implementation. accumulate_downstream() now builds topological-sort graph via igraph::graph_from_data_frame() consistent id/toid handling. Add testthat scaffold topology GeoPackage-metadata round-trip tests. Lynker Spatial pkgdown site cross-stack navigation.
